executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The runtime is cleaner now, but blocked-channel recovery and loop-verifier contracts are still only partially normalized, so ownership and independent-signoff rules remain unevenly enforced.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Give blocked-channel recovery one explicit runtime contract: either one owner schedule or separate deep-review/followup entrypoints with distinct verifier evidence.

findings.title: Blocked-channel recovery still has two schedules driving one code path
findings.mechanism: `blocked-channel-deep-review` and `blocked-channel-followup` are now correctly unblocker-owned, but both still invoke the same unittest + `agents/unblocker/run.py` path without distinct verifier artifacts or clearly separate runtime contracts.
findings.root_cause: The ownership rename happened faster than the runtime split, so cadence differences exist without code-level role separation.
findings.recommended_fix: Either collapse both schedules into one unblocker owner loop or create distinct deep-review/followup entrypoints plus separate verifier evidence so overlap becomes intentional and auditable.

findings.title: Most live repair/watchdog loops are only tracked in metadata, not enforced by full checker/runner/verifier contracts
findings.mechanism: `self_improvement_loops.json` now maps the live loop topology, but marketing, Ralph-Site, system-health, blocked-channel recovery, and the architecture watchdog are still `tracked_only`, so the stronger AGENTS rule exists mainly as governance text rather than a uniform runtime contract.
findings.root_cause: New runtime-owned loops were added faster than the full self-improvement contract was generalized beyond the docs stack.
findings.recommended_fix: Promote each true self-improvement loop to a full contract with checker/runner/verifier artifacts, or explicitly classify it as monitoring/owner-only so the rule is enforced by schema rather than interpretation.

findings.title: OS-cron drift was repaired, but there is still no explicit allowlist guard against future non-Gateway scheduler creep
findings.mechanism: Legacy HireAegis/content/community/product cron entries and the OS-cron mirror-sync entry were removed this run, but no current audit step fails hard when unexpected user crontab entries reappear.
findings.root_cause: Scheduler ownership cleanup happened reactively instead of being enforced by an explicit runtime invariant.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a small allowlist check for user crontab contents so future hidden scheduler layers fail the audit immediately instead of waiting for manual discovery.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Normalize blocked-channel recovery into one explicit runtime contract
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: It is the largest remaining overlap in the live repair topology.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Cleaner ownership, less duplicate recovery churn, and clearer verifier expectations.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Extend the loop registry schema so non-docs loops are either fully contracted or explicitly non-self-improving
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The registry now reflects live topology, but its enforcement strength is still uneven.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Machine-auditable verifier requirements instead of prompt-only governance.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Add an OS-crontab allowlist invariant to architecture/loop-integrity auditing
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This run found and repaired hidden scheduler drift that could otherwise return silently.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Future scheduler contamination gets caught immediately.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Kept the prior boundary fix in place: the system health monitor remains limited to cron/runtime health and does not own marketing-state remediation.
Renamed the misleading Reddit-branded unblocker job to `blocked-channel-followup` and clarified both unblocker job descriptions as generic blocked-channel ownership.
Normalized the disabled aggressive docs self-heal job into explicit legacy/deactivated language so it no longer reads like the active authority.
Stopped the loop-integrity audit from restoring `REMOVED:` OS-cron docs entries and expanded the registry to track live repair/watchdog loops with explicit contract modes.
Removed legacy HireAegis/content/community/product OS-cron entries, migrated Codeberg→GitHub mirror sync into Gateway cron as `codeberg-github-mirror-sync`, and cleared the old OS-cron sync entry.
Removed the extreme 'human intervention forbidden' wording and replaced it with fail-closed proof and handoff rules.

independent_verification.summary: A fresh isolated verifier reran the repaired architecture checks and passed after the Gateway mirror-sync migration and crontab cleanup.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Shared blocked-channel code path with split schedules but no split verifier contract
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: The ownership labels are now cleaner, but the runtime still has two cadences feeding one recovery implementation without distinct proof boundaries.
